stem: Let \( f(x,y) = [[A]]x^{2} + [[B]]y^{2} + [[C]]x + [[D]]y \). Using the tangent plane to \( z=f(x,y) \) at \( ([[x0]],[[y0]]) \) to approximate \( f \), what value does this tangent plane give at \( (x,y)=([[x1]],[[y1]]) \)?
distractor_rationales: This evaluates the actual (curved) function \( f \) at \( ([[x1]],[[y1]]) \), not the linear tangent-plane approximation.
This reports \( f([[x0]],[[y0]]) \) itself, forgetting the linear correction terms.
This drops the base value \( f([[x0]],[[y0]]) \), keeping only the correction terms.
This swaps the roles of \( f_x \) and \( f_y \) in the two correction terms.
This is off by two from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.

stem: Let \( f(x,y) = [[A]]x^{2}y + [[B]]x + [[C]]y \). The tangent plane to \( z=f(x,y) \) at \( ([[x0]],[[y0]]) \) can be written \( z = f([[x0]],[[y0]]) + m(x-[[x0]]) + n(y-[[y0]]) \). What is \( m \), the coefficient of \( (x-[[x0]]) \)?
solution: The coefficient \( m \) is the partial derivative \( f_x([[x0]],[[y0]]) \). Holding \( y=[[y0]] \) fixed, \( f(x,[[y0]]) = [[A]]([[y0]])x^{2} + [[B]]x + [[C]]([[y0]]) \), so \( f_x(x,[[y0]]) = 2[[A]]([[y0]])x + [[B]] \). Evaluating at \( x=[[x0]] \) gives \( m = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is \( n \), the coefficient of \( y \) (i.e. \( f_y([[x0]],[[y0]]) \)), not \( m \).
This reports \( f([[x0]],[[y0]]) \) itself, not the tangent plane's \( x \)-coefficient.
This omits the constant term \( [[B]] \) from the derivative.
This treats \( f \) as if the \( x^2y \) term's derivative had no factor of 2.
This is off by two from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.

stem: Let \( f(x,y) = [[A]]y^{3} + [[B]]xy \). The tangent plane to \( z=f(x,y) \) at \( ([[x0]],[[y0]]) \) can be written \( z = f([[x0]],[[y0]]) + m(x-[[x0]]) + n(y-[[y0]]) \). What is \( n \), the coefficient of \( (y-[[y0]]) \)?
solution: The coefficient \( n \) is the partial derivative \( f_y([[x0]],[[y0]]) \). Holding \( x=[[x0]] \) fixed, \( f([[x0]],y) = [[A]]y^{3} + [[B]]([[x0]])y \), so \( f_y([[x0]],y) = 3[[A]]y^{2} + [[B]]([[x0]]) \). Evaluating at \( y=[[y0]] \) gives \( n = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is \( m \), the coefficient of \( x \) (i.e. \( f_x([[x0]],[[y0]]) \)), not \( n \).
This reports \( f([[x0]],[[y0]]) \) itself, not the tangent plane's \( y \)-coefficient.
This drops the factor of 3 on the \( y^{3} \) term's derivative.
This forgets to cube (rather than square) the correct power in the \( y^3 \) term's derivative and mishandles the constant.
This is off by three from the correct value.
This is off by three from the correct value.

stem: Let \( f(x,y) = [[A]]x^{2} + [[B]]xy + [[C]]y^{2} + [[E]]x + [[F]]y \). The tangent plane to \( z=f(x,y) \) at \( ([[x0]],[[y0]]) \) crosses the \( z \)-axis at \( z = k \) (i.e. its value at \( (x,y)=(0,0) \)). What is \( k \)?
distractor_rationales: This reports \( f([[x0]],[[y0]]) \) itself, forgetting the correction from moving to \( (0,0) \).
This drops the base value \( f([[x0]],[[y0]]) \), keeping only the (positive) correction terms.
This has the wrong overall sign on the function value term.
This drops the \( y \)-correction term entirely.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Let \( f(x,y) = [[A]]\sin(x) + [[B]]y^{2} \). Using the tangent plane to \( z=f(x,y) \) at \( (0,[[y0]]) \) to approximate \( f \), what value does this tangent plane give at \( (x,y)=([[x1]],[[y1]]) \)?
distractor_rationales: This evaluates the actual (curved) function \( f \) at \( ([[x1]],[[y1]]) \), not the linear tangent-plane approximation.
This reports \( f(0,[[y0]]) \) itself, forgetting the linear correction terms.
This drops the base value \( f(0,[[y0]]) \), keeping only the correction terms.
This mistakenly multiplies the \( x \)-correction by \( [[y0]] \), an extra unwanted factor.
This is off by two from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.
